title: Reggae on the Rooftop Festival — Juneteenth Weekend
location: AIR Rooftop (formerly Suite Lounge), 75 Luckie St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Six-hour rooftop reggae / dancehall / Soca / Afrobeats festival with skyline views, complimentary jerk chicken and rum punch (while supplies last). The easiest 'show up and have fun' date on the calendar — split a drink, find shade, vibe out.

title: AFRO CARIBBEAN FEST Rooftop Party (Free til 11 PM)
location: Cafe Circa, 464 Edgewood Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Late-night Afrobeats, dancehall, reggae and Soca on a rooftop with international DJs and skyline views. Zero-cost entry makes this the no-brainer late date — show up after dinner and dance until the lights come up.

title: Happy People Atlanta (Culture & Vibes Festival)
location: Underground Atlanta, 50 Upper Alabama St SW, Atlanta, GA
description: All-day Afrocentric culture festival with music, vendors and food. Built for wandering together — great for a first date where you want options to stay or bounce.

title: R&B Brunch: Summer of Soul at City Winery
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Brunch, wine and live R&B — the laziest, most romantic version of a date. Eat, sip, and let the music do the flirting for you.

title: Salsa at Politan Row — Bachata & Social
location: Politan Row at Colony Square, 1197 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Salsa and bachata lessons followed by open social dancing in a food-hall courtyard. Grab tacos and a margarita, then dance — one-stop Sunday afternoon date with dinner built in.

title: Free Salsa & Bachata Lessons at El Super Pan
location: El Super Pan @ The Battery Atlanta, 800 Battery Ave SE
description: Complimentary group lessons in salsa and bachata at the Latin kitchen inside The Battery. Pair with a Braves game or a Battery stroll afterwards for an easy Sunday date.

title: Vegan Soul Sunday — Real Men Cook Vegan (Father's Day Edition)
location: Piedmont Park area, Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Plant-based soul-food pop-up with live music and a community vibe. Date-friendly if you both love trying new vegan plates side-by-side.

title: Seeds of Resistance: An Opening Gallery Show
location: Southern Fried Queer Pride venue, Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Opening-night group exhibition tied to Southern Fried Queer Pride — strong conversation-starter energy, perfect for a couples night where you want to actually talk afterward over coffee or wine.

title: Plant to the Future: Food, Freedom & Black Futures Conference
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Plant-based food justice conference with food demos, panels and tastings. For the couple whose perfect date is learning something together and leaving with a full stomach.

title: LAKŌU Atlanta — Haitian Cultural Festival
location: Westside Motor Lounge, 725 Echo St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Haitian culture, food and music all day at Westside Motor Lounge with World Cup energy. Eat together, dance together — basically a date that runs from lunch to last call.

title: R.A.S World Cup Atlanta — 100% Reggae, Afrobeat, Soca
location: Air Rooftop Bar Atl, 1114 W Peachtree St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Late-night Caribbean party — DJs spinning nothing but reggae, Afrobeats and Soca on a rooftop. Easy 'wear something cute and dance' date.

title: Shakira — Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour
location: State Farm Arena, 1 State Farm Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Shakira's World Cup Edition arena tour — big, glittery, loud. Best for the couple who wants a proper 'splurge night' with dancing in the aisles.

title: A Taste of Luxury by Chef Brittany
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Chef-curated tasting experience — perfect 'anniversary-adjacent' date for couples who eat out for the experience, not just the food.

title: Best of Atlanta Comedy Showcase
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Curated local comedians — the kind of date where you leave with inside jokes you'll bring up for months.

title: Mo Amer at Helium Comedy Club
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: Mo Amer (Netflix, 'Mo') — smart, personal, generous comedy. Pair with dinner in Alpharetta for a full Friday night.

title: Get Up & Dance Latin Social
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Latin dance social — bachata, salsa, merengue. Built for two-stepping and side-stepping your nerves away.

title: An Evening of Soul: Anthony Hamilton & Musiq Soulchild
location: Mable House Barnes Amphitheatre, 5239 Floyd Rd, Mableton, GA
description: Two neo-soul heavyweights under the stars at an outdoor amphitheatre. Date-night canon — picnic, blanket, slow-dance songs from your middle-school playlist.

title: Feast Your Eyes: Solo Exhibition by Sir Nicholas Lucius
location: 1401 Peachtree St NE Ste A200, Atlanta, GA
description: Opening-night solo exhibition on Peachtree — gallery stroll plus a bar equals an easy, low-pressure 'we have taste' date.

title: Vinyl Sessions: June Edition
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Vinyl-DJ curated listening night — a date where you're both discovering new (old) songs at the same time.

title: T. Murph at City Winery Atlanta
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Comedy + wine + a sit-down room = one of the easiest 'talk-and-laugh' dates in the city.

title: Dinner and A Date: A Night of Soul Live Music
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Dinner + live soul music — literally billed as a date night. Bring someone you've been wanting to slow-dance with.

title: Jokes & Cheese Clean Comedy Show
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Clean comedy paired with cheese — a wholesome date idea for early stages where you don't want blue material.

title: GAZE — Black LGBTQ+ Artists Group Show
location: The Bakery Atlanta, Atlanta, GA
description: 11-artist Pride group show — walk, look, talk, repeat. Coffee or wine after turns a 45-minute gallery stop into a real date.

title: This Is Atlanta — Landmark Exhibition (VINSONart)
location: VINSONart Gallery, Atlanta, GA
description: Anchor Atlanta art show running all summer — a flexible 'any weekend works' date option with a clear conversation piece.

title: From Afrique, With Love — Artemus Jenkins Photo Exhibition
location: Atlanta, GA (see listing)
description: Photography show rooted in African and African-diasporic storytelling — strong match for a thoughtful couples afternoon.
